stem:
y = 6x + 3

One of the two equations in a system of linear equations is given. The system has infinitely many solutions. Which equation could be the second equation in this system?
explanation: A linear system has infinitely many solutions only when the two equations are equivalent. Multiplying both sides of y = 6x + 3 by 2 gives 2(y) = 2(6x + 3), which is the same line.
